extraction.one_line_summary: BENNETT Sam won a flat sprint at Breda with decisive advantage from VAN POPPEL Danny's leadout in final 200m after sharp right-hand corner with 800m remaining.
tactical_patterns: VAN POPPEL Danny's leadout timing at 200m post-corner proved decisive; teams without dedicated final pilot struggled with positioning panic.
ACKERMANN Pascal went early from distance without support, exposing weakness of isolated sprinters on technical finishes with late directional changes.
MERLIER Tim's team did significant work but he was incorrectly positioned at corner exit, demonstrating leadout execution matters more than total effort.

rider_signals.BENNETT Sam: positive: two consecutive wins with VAN POPPEL Danny providing superior final 200m piloting compared to rivals lacking dedicated last man
rider_signals.PEDERSEN Mads: positive: consecutive second places show patience through late corners rather than panic when initially 10-12 positions back post-turn
rider_signals.MERLIER Tim: negative: team worked extensively but rider positioning at corner exit incorrect two days running, suggesting either tactical error or form issue
rider_signals.ACKERMANN Pascal: negative: initiated sprint prematurely without leadout support, burning matches before corner rather than exploiting VAN POPPEL Danny vacuum
rider_signals.AFFINI Edoardo: neutral: track specialist's team pursuit power engine critical to TTT success but acknowledges mountain stages will extract cost from 190lb frame
rider_signals.VAN POPPEL Danny: positive: delivered BENNETT Sam perfectly positioned at 200m marker, demonstrating elite leadout timing through technical right-hand corner sequence
rider_signals.VANDENBROECK Julius: neutral: attacked immediately for KOM jersey points with strategic awareness that second place suffices to retain polka dots under Vuelta scoring
